medications.indication_rationale: First-line for a mild auricular relapsing polychondritis flare, if no contraindication; reduces cartilage inflammation and pain.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g. ibuprofen 400 mg PO with food TDS as needed (adult, if no contraindication)

medications.indication_rationale: Used for persistent/severe or systemic relapsing polychondritis, but initiated by the assessing clinician/rheumatology after excluding infection — not blindly via telehealth.
No clear infection now; antibiotics are a decision for the in-person assessor if perichondritis is suspected.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Apply a cold compress (wrapped) to the ear, sleep on the opposite side, avoid heat/rubbing/massage of the ear, and watch for the safety-net features.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces cartilage inflammation and pain while enabling escalation if infection/airway features develop.

patient_education_points.point: The fact that your soft earlobe is completely normal and only the firm cartilage part is inflamed strongly suggests this is your cartilage-inflammation condition (relapsing polychondritis), not a spreading skin infection or brain infection.

Take an anti-inflammatory with food, use a cold compress, sleep on the other side, and don't apply heat or rub the ear. Because a cartilage infection can't be fully ruled out over video, please be seen in person today or first thing tomorrow.

Go to ED immediately if the redness spreads onto your face/neck or involves the earlobe, you get swelling/pain behind the ear over the bone with the ear pushed forward, a high fever, or any hoarseness or breathing difficulty.

follow_up_plan.interval: urgent same/next-day in-person assessment (GP/ENT); rheumatology review
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Exclude bacterial perichondritis/mastoiditis, assess relapsing polychondritis activity and systemic/airway involvement, consider corticosteroids.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Spreading/lobe involvement, post-auricular swelling, fever, hoarseness or breathing difficulty — ED.

must_include_items: Recognise earlobe sparing as pointing to relapsing polychondritis over infection
Arrange urgent same/next-day in-person assessment (cartilage infection not excludable remotely)
acceptable_alternatives: Corticosteroids initiated by the assessing clinician if infection excluded
errors_of_omission: No in-person assessment or infection/airway safety-net
errors_of_commission: Home-only reassurance without excluding cartilage infection
Applying heat/massage to the inflamed cartilage
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: High-risk case: passing requires urgent in-person assessment (perichondritis/mastoiditis not excludable remotely) PLUS an airway/mastoiditis safety-net. Home-only reassurance is a failure.
